scenario.title: Claim filed for device with partially illegible serial number
scenario.description: Customer requests an accidental damage claim after dropping their iPhone, but the physical serial number is partially scratched off and cannot be fully confirmed, and the device does not power on to retrieve the electronic serial.

service_fee.notes: Service fee only applies if Apple accepts alternate proof; otherwise, claim is denied.

policy_references.section: 5. How to Obtain Service and Support?
policy_references.snippet: If you call, you will need to give the Apple technical support representative who answers your call the Plan Agreement Number or Covered iPhone serial number for your iPhone before you can receive assistance.

The Plan does not apply to an iPhone with a serial number that has been altered, defaced or removed, or has been modified to alter its functionality or capability without the written permission of the manufacturer.
You will provide your Plan Agreement Number and a copy of your Plan’s original proof of purchase, if requested.

scenario.reasoning: The policy generally excludes devices with altered or defaced serial numbers (Section 4.2(iii)), but it is ambiguous when damage is accidental and not done to conceal identity. Section 5 and Section 7(i) contemplate alternate forms of verification (plan agreement number, proof of purchase). Judgment is required to determine if alternate proofs reasonably tie the device to coverage, especially when accidental damage prevented serial confirmation.

persona.defining quirks: Enjoys painting in his free time
persona.household type: Married couple household, no children of the householder less than 18
persona.personal time: Spends free time painting or reading

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario description:
Customer requests an accidental damage claim after dropping their iPhone, but the physical serial number is partially scratched off and cannot be fully confirmed, and the device does not power on to retrieve the electronic serial.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):

}

  },

  "detailed job description": "Retired artist",
  "disability": "None",
  "education": "Bachelor's degree",
  "employment status": "Not in labor force",
  "family presence and age": "No related children",
  "health insurance": "With health insurance coverage",

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hello, I hope you're doing well. I need to file a claim for my iPhone 14 that I accidentally dropped. Unfortunately, the device won’t power on, and the serial number on the back is partially scratched off, so I can't provide the full number. How should I proceed?
messages.raw_text: Hello, I hope you're doing well. I need to file a claim for my iPhone 14 that I accidentally dropped. Unfortunately, the device won’t power on, and the serial number on the back is partially scratched off, so I can't provide the full number. How should I proceed?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: I’m sorry to hear about your iPhone 14. To start your claim, I need either your Plan Agreement Number or the full iPhone Serial Number to validate your coverage. If the serial number is unreadable, do you have your Plan Agreement Number available?
messages.raw_text: I’m sorry to hear about your iPhone 14. To start your claim, I need either your Plan Agreement Number or the full iPhone Serial Number to validate your coverage. If the serial number is unreadable, do you have your Plan Agreement Number available?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yes, I do have my Plan Agreement Number. It’s APLCARE-5137890412. Please let me know what else you need from me to move forward.
messages.raw_text: Yes, I do have my Plan Agreement Number. It’s APLCARE-5137890412. Please let me know what else you need from me to move forward.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing your Plan Agreement Number. Could you please describe the issue with your iPhone in more detail? For example, any visible damage, specific symptoms, or error messages you noticed.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing your Plan Agreement Number. Could you please describe the issue with your iPhone in more detail? For example, any visible damage, specific symptoms, or error messages you noticed.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Certainly. I accidentally dropped my iPhone 14 on a hard surface, and now it won’t turn on at all. There’s a noticeable dent on the bottom right corner, and the screen is cracked, but there are no error messages since the device does not power up. I’ve tried charging it, but nothing happens.
messages.raw_text: Certainly. I accidentally dropped my iPhone 14 on a hard surface, and now it won’t turn on at all. There’s a noticeable dent on the bottom right corner, and the screen is cracked, but there are no error messages since the device does not power up. I’ve tried charging it, but nothing happens.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the details. To better assist you, could you please confirm the iPhone model and the iOS version it was running before the issue? Also, let me know if any peripherals were connected at the time or if you have tried any troubleshooting steps.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the details. To better assist you, could you please confirm the iPhone model and the iOS version it was running before the issue? Also, let me know if any peripherals were connected at the time or if you have tried any troubleshooting steps.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: The model is iPhone 14 with 128 GB storage. As for the iOS version, I believe it was running iOS 16, since I make sure to keep my device updated. I didn’t have any peripherals connected at the time. For troubleshooting, I tried charging it with different cables and power sources, but still no response. I haven’t attempted any resets since it’s completely unresponsive.
messages.raw_text: The model is iPhone 14 with 128 GB storage. As for the iOS version, I believe it was running iOS 16, since I make sure to keep my device updated. I didn’t have any peripherals connected at the time. For troubleshooting, I tried charging it with different cables and power sources, but still no response. I haven’t attempted any resets since it’s completely unresponsive.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the information. Since this issue appears to be caused by the drop, could you please provide more details about the accident? Specifically, where and when it happened, and how the drop occurred.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the information. Since this issue appears to be caused by the drop, could you please provide more details about the accident? Specifically, where and when it happened, and how the drop occurred.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Of course. The accident happened yesterday afternoon in my art studio. I was reaching for some paint supplies on a high shelf when the phone slipped from my hand and fell about three feet onto the hardwood floor. The impact was directly on the bottom right corner, which explains the dent and the cracked screen. I immediately tried to turn it on, but it wouldn’t power up.
messages.raw_text: Of course. The accident happened yesterday afternoon in my art studio. I was reaching for some paint supplies on a high shelf when the phone slipped from my hand and fell about three feet onto the hardwood floor. The impact was directly on the bottom right corner, which explains the dent and the cracked screen. I immediately tried to turn it on, but it wouldn’t power up.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the accident details. Your claim will now be processed based on the information provided. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the accident details. Your claim will now be processed based on the information provided. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that covers everything for now. I appreciate your help in getting this claim started. Have a pleasant day.
messages.raw_text: No, that covers everything for now. I appreciate your help in getting this claim started. Have a pleasant day.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. Your claim will be processed, and you will be notified once it is complete. Have a great day!
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. Your claim will be processed, and you will be notified once it is complete. Have a great day! <END>
